This avoids disk I/O on every classification.\n219\t        \"\"\"\n220\t        try:\n221\t            return self._get_reload_manager().get_prompt(\"router\")\n222\t        except Exception as e:\n223\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to load router prompt: {e}\")\n224\t            return _ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK\n225\t\n226\t    def _build_system_prompt(self) -> str:\n227\t        \"\"\"Build the system prompt from the router segmentation prompt.\n228\t\n229\t        Latency optimization: Removed urgency prompt splicing from hot path.\n230\t        The router still classifies urgency based on core intent patterns,\n231\t        without the extra token overhead of separate urgency rules.\n232\t        \"\"\"\n233\t        return self._load_router_prompt()\n234\t\n235\t    async def classify_utterance(\n236\t        self,\n237\t        utterance: str,\n238\t        session_id: str,\n239\t        retry_on_malformed: bool = True,\n240\t    ) -> list[IntentClassification]:\n241\t        \"\"\"\n242\t        Classify an utterance into intents.\n243\t\n244\t        Uses LLM to segment and classify the utterance.\n245\t\n246\t        Args:\n247\t            utterance: The user utterance\n248\t            session_id: Session ID for context\n249\t            retry_on_malformed: If True, perform one corrective retry on JSON parse failure\n250\t\n251\t        Returns:\n252\t            List of IntentClassification objects\n253\t\n254\t        Raises:\n255\t            RouterTimeoutError: LLM call timed out\n256\t            RouterQuotaError: LLM quota exhausted\n257\t            RouterProxyError: LLM proxy unreachable\n258\t            RouterMalformedError: Malformed JSON after corrective retry\n259\t        \"\"\"\n260\t        # Return empty timing breakdown for cached results (no actual LLM call was made)\n264\t            empty_breakdown = {\n265\t                \"cached\": True,\n266\t                \"prompt_construction_ms\": 0,\n267\t                \"proxy_call_ms\": 0,\n268\t                \"proxy_network_ms\": 0,\n269\t                \"proxy_inference_ms\": 0,\n270\t                \"json_parse_ms\": 0,\n271\t                \"process_ms\": 0,\n272\t                \"total_ms\": 0,\n273\t                \"intents_count\": len(cached_result),\n274\t            }\n275\t            return cached_result, empty_breakdown\n276\t\n277\t        # Track retry attempt for malformed JSON\n278\t        retry_count = 0\n279\t        raw_response = None\n280\t\n281\t        try:\n282\t            # Measure total classification time\n283\t            classify_start = time.perf_counter()\n284\t\n285\t            # Use dedicated router client with 10s timeout for fail-fast behavior\n286\t            client = await self._get_router_zai_client()\n287\t\n288\t            # Measure prompt construction time (template rendering, message formatting)\n289\t            # This isolates client-side preparation cost from network and inference time\n290\t            prompt_start = time.perf_counter()\n291\t\n292\t            # Build user message\n293\t            user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}\"\n294\t\n295\t            logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n296\t\n297\t            # Build system prompt from prompts/router.md\n298\t            # (hot-reload enabled via reload manager)\n299\t            system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt()\n300\t\n301\t            prompt_ms = (time.perf_counter() - prompt_start) * 1000\n302\t\n303\t            # Measure ZAI proxy call time (network + model inference)\n304\t            # The LLM client now returns timing breakdown separating network latency from model inference\n305\t            proxy_start = time.perf_counter()\n306\t            response_data = await client.call_simple(\n307\t                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n308\t                user_message=user_message,\n309\t                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n310\t                max_tokens=128,  # OPTIMIZATION 2: Reduced from 256 to 128 - intent JSON is typically 50-80 tokens\n311\t                temperature=0.0,\n312\t                return_timing=True,  # Request timing breakdown from LLM client\n313\t            )\n314\t            proxy_ms = (time.perf_counter() - proxy_start) * 1000\n315\t\n316\t            # Extract content and timing from response\n317\t            # response_data is now a dict with content and timing breakdown\n318\t            response = response_data.get(\"content\", \"\")\n319\t            timing_network_ms = response_data.get(\"timing_network_ms\")\n320\t            timing_inference_ms = response_data.get(\"timing_inference_ms\")\n321\t\n322\t            # Store raw response for error reporting\n323\t            raw_response = response\n324\t\n325\t            # Calculate model inference time: proxy response time - network time\n326\t            # This gives us the actual model inference duration by subtracting network RTT\n327\t            calculated_inference_ms = None\n328\t            if timing_network_ms is not None:\n329\t                calculated_inference_ms = proxy_ms - timing_network_ms\n330\t\n331\t            # Measure JSON parsing time\n332\t            parse_start = time.perf_counter()\n333\t            try:\n334\t                # Optimized JSON parsing using centralized parser\n335\t                # Uses single-pass find()/rfind() approach (faster than split/rsplit)\n336\t                # Handles GLM-4.7's markdown-fenced responses efficiently\n337\t                # See src/llm/response_parser.py for implementation\n338\t                intents_data = parse_llm_response(response, strip_fences=True, expect_json=True)\n339\t            except ParseLLMError as e:\n340\t                # Already has raw_response attached; just re-raise\n341\t                raise e\n342\t            parse_ms = (time.perf_counter() - parse_start) * 1000\n343\t\n344\t            # Measure classification processing time\n345\t            process_start = time.perf_counter()\n346\t            classifications = []\n347\t\n348\t            for intent_data in intents_data:\n349\t                # Map string to IntentType enum\n350\t                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n351\t                try:\n352\t                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n353\t                except ValueError:\n354\t                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n355\t                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n356\t\n357\t                classification = IntentClassification(\n358\t                    intent_type=intent_type,\n359\t                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n360\t                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n361\t                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n362\t                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n363\t                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n364\t                    lookup_kind=intent_data.get(\"lookup_kind\") if intent_type == IntentType.LOOKUP else None,\n365\t                )\n366\t                classifications.append(classification)\n367\t            process_ms = (time.perf_counter() - process_start) * 1000\n368\t\n369\t            total_ms = (time.perf_counter() - classify_start) * 1000\n370\t\n371\t            # Log detailed timing breakdown for latency profiling\n372\t            # This shows WHERE the 1,587-2,074ms latency is spent across the 4 investigation areas\n373\t            network_str = f\"{timing_network_ms:.2f}\" if timing_network_ms is not None else \"N/A\"\n374\t            calculated_inference_str = f\"{calculated_inference_ms:.2f}\" if calculated_inference_ms is not None else \"N/A\"\n375\t            logger.info(\n376\t                f\"router_timing breakdown: \"\n377\t                f\"prompt_construction_ms={prompt_ms:.2f} \"\n378\t                f\"proxy_call_ms={proxy_ms:.2f} \"\n379\t                f\"proxy_network_ms={network_str} \"\n380\t                f\"proxy_inference_ms={calculated_inference_str} \"\n381\t                f\"json_parse_ms={parse_ms:.2f} \"\n382\t                f\"process_ms={process_ms:.2f} \"\n383\t                f\"total_ms={total_ms:.2f} \"\n384\t                f\"intents={len(classifications)}\"\n385\t            )\n386\t\n387\t            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n388\t\n389\t            # Build timing breakdown for storage\n390\t            # Now includes separate network and inference timing measured by LLM client\n391\t            timing_breakdown = {\n392\t                \"prompt_construction_ms\": round(prompt_ms, 2),\n393\t                \"proxy_call_ms\": round(proxy_ms, 2),  # Total round-trip time (network + inference)\n394\t                \"proxy_network_ms\": round(timing_network_ms, 2) if timing_network_ms is not None else None,\n395\t                \"proxy_inference_ms\": round(calculated_inference_ms, 2) if calculated_inference_ms is not None else None,\n396\t                \"json_parse_ms\": round(parse_ms, 2),\n397\t                \"process_ms\": round(process_ms, 2),\n398\t                \"total_ms\": round(total_ms, 2),\n399\t                \"intents_count\": len(classifications),\n400\t            }\n401\t\n402\t            # OPTIMIZATION 1 (cont): Cache successful classifications\n403\t            self._cache_classification(utterance, session_id, classifications)\n404\t\n405\t            return classifications, timing_breakdown\n406\t\n407\t        except ParseLLMError as e:\n408\t            \"\"\"\n409\t            First parsing attempt failed - implement corrective retry.\n410\t\n411\t            Router uses corrective retry because it's the first step in the pipeline.\n412\t            A malformed router response cascades to everything downstream.\n413\t            One retry is justified because it's cheap (same prompt, 2048 tokens).\n414\t            \"\"\"\n415\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response: {e}\")\n416\t            raw_output = e.raw_response or raw_response  # Preserve for error reporting\n417\t\n418\t            # Implement one corrective retry on malformed JSON\n419\t            if retry_on_malformed and retry_count == 0:\n420\t                logger.info(\"Malformed JSON detected, attempting corrective retry...\")\n421\t                retry_count += 1\n422\t                try:\n423\t                    # Retry once with same parameters\n424\t                    classifications, _ = await self.classify_utterance(\n425\t                        utterance=utterance,\n426\t                        session_id=session_id,\n427\t                        retry_on_malformed=False,  # Prevent infinite retry\n428\t                    )\n429\t                    return classifications, _\n430\t                except ParseLLMError as retry_e:\n431\t                    # Retry also failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n432\t                    raise RouterMalformedError(\n433\t                        parse_error=str(retry_e),\n434\t                        raw_output=retry_e.raw_response or raw_response,\n435\t                        retry_count=retry_count,\n436\t                    ) from retry_e\n437\t\n438\t            # No retry allowed or retry failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n439\t            raise RouterMalformedError(\n440\t                parse_error=str(e),\n441\t                raw_output=raw_output,\n442\t                retry_count=retry_count,\n443\t            ) from e\n444\t\n445\t        except (LLMTimeoutError, asyncio.TimeoutError) as e:\n446\t            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\")\n447\t            raise RouterTimeoutError(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\") from e\n448\t\n449\t        except (LLMRateLimitError,) as e:\n450\t            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\")\n451\t            raise RouterQuotaError(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\") from e\n452\t\n453\t        except (LLMError, httpx.HTTPError) as e:\n454\t            logger.error(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\")\n455\t            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\") from e\n456\t\n457\t        except Exception as e:\n458\t            logger.error(f\"Classification failed with unexpected error: {e}\")\n459\t            # Wrap unknown errors as RouterProxyError for graceful degradation\n460\t            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM call failed: {e}\") from e\n461\t\n462\t    async def route_utterance(\n463\t        self,\n464\t        utterance: str,\n465\t        utterance_id: str,\n466\t        session_id: str,\n467\t    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n468\t        \"\"\"\n469\t        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n470\t\n471\t        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n472\t\n473\t        Args:\n474\t            utterance: The user utterance\n475\t            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n476\t            session_id: Session ID\n477\t\n478\t        Returns:\n479\t            List of RoutedIntent objects\n480\t\n481\t        Raises:\n482\t            RouterError: If router fails and broadcasts error event\n483\t        \"\"\"\n484\t        # Generate a fallback intent_id for error reporting\n485\t        error_intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n486\t\n487\t        try:\n488\t            # Classify the utterance (one LLM call per utterance — the router stage\n489\t            # is shared across every intent thread it produces, so its duration is\n490\t            # measured once here and stamped onto each RoutedIntent).\n491\t            classify_start = time.monotonic()\n492\t            classifications, timing_breakdown = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n493\t            router_ms = int((time.monotonic() - classify_start) * 1000)\n494\t\n495\t            # Store router timing breakdown in utterance record\n496\t            try:\n497\t                store = await self._get_store()\n498\t                await store.update_utterance_router_timing(utterance_id, timing_breakdown)\n499\t                logger.info(f\"Stored router timing breakdown for utterance {utterance_id[:8]}\")\n500\t            except Exception as e:\n501\t                logger.warning(f\"Failed to store router timing breakdown: {e}\")\n502\t                # Non-fatal: continue with routing\n503\t\n504\t            routed_intents = []\n505\t            for classification in classifications:\n506\t                # Create intent ID\n507\t                intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n508\t\n509\t                routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n510\t                    intent_id=intent_id,\n511\t                    classification=classification,\n512\t                    session_id=session_id,\n513\t                    utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n514\t                    router_ms=router_ms,\n515\t                )\n516\t                routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n517\t\n518\t            return routed_intents\n519\t\n520\t        except RouterMalformedError as e:\n521\t            # Malformed JSON after corrective retry - broadcast clarification card\n522\t            logger.error(f\"Router malformed after retry: {e.parse_error}\")\n523\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n524\t            await handler.broadcast_clarification_card(\n525\t                utterance=utterance,\n526\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n527\t                session_id=session_id,\n528\t                parse_error=e.parse_error,\n529\t                retry_count=e.retry_count,\n530\t                raw_output_snippet=e.raw_output[:200] if e.raw_output else None,\n531\t            )\n532\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n533\t\n534\t        except RouterTimeoutError as e:\n535\t            # Router LLM timeout - broadcast router_unavailable\n536\t            logger.error(f\"Router timeout: {e}\")\n537\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n538\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n539\t                utterance=utterance,\n540\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n541\t                session_id=session_id,\n542\t                error_reason=\"timeout\",\n543\t            )\n544\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n545\t\n546\t        except RouterQuotaError as e:\n547\t            # Router quota exhausted - broadcast router_unavailable\n548\t            logger.error(f\"Router quota exhausted: {e}\")\n549\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n550\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n551\t                utterance=utterance,\n552\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n553\t                session_id=session_id,\n554\t                error_reason=\"quota_exhausted\",\n555\t            )\n556\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n557\t\n558\t        except RouterProxyError as e:\n559\t            # Router proxy unreachable - broadcast router_unavailable\n560\t            logger.error(f\"Router proxy error: {e}\")\n561\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n562\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n563\t                utterance=utterance,\n564\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n565\t                session_id=session_id,\n566\t                error_reason=\"proxy_down\",\n567\t            )\n568\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n569\t\n570\t    async def process_intent(\n571\t        self,\n572\t        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n573\t    ) -> dict:\n574\t        \"\"\"\n575\t        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n576\t\n577\t        Args:\n578\t            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n579\t\n580\t        Returns:\n581\t            Result dictionary with status and data\n582\t        \"\"\"\n583\t        classification = routed_intent.classification\n584\t\n585\t        # Capture per-stage timings for this dispatch and persist one\n586\t        # dispatch_timings row (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Each\n587\t        # branch records the stages it measures; router_ms is shared across\n588\t        # the utterance's threads and was stamped on the RoutedIntent. Capture\n589\t        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\n590\t        # the dispatch itself.\n591\t        timings = DispatchTimings()\n592\t        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n593\t\n594\t        # Honesty guards for unimplemented intents\n595\t        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.ACTION:\n596\t            # Action executor is NOT BUILT — broadcast design-only card\n597\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n598\t            await handler.broadcast_action_design_only(\n599\t                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n600\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n601\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n602\t                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n603\t            )\n604\t\n605\t            # Update intent status to reflect the design-only state\n606\t            store = await self._get_store()\n607\t            await store.update_intent_status(\n608\t                routed_intent.intent_id,\n609\t                \"resolved\",\n610\t                \"Action execution is design-only — executor not built\",\n611\t            )\n612\t\n613\t            return {\n614\t                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n615\t                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n616\t                \"status\": \"design_only\",\n617\t                \"message\": \"Action execution is not yet available\",\n618\t            }\n619\t\n620\t        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.REMINDER:\n621\t            # Reminders are NOT YET IMPLEMENTED — broadcast clarification card\n622\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n623\t            await handler.broadcast_reminder_unavailable(\n624\t                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n625\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n626\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n627\t            )\n628\t\n629\t            # Update intent status to reflect that reminders are unavailable\n630\t            store = await self._get_store()\n631\t            await store.update_intent_status(\n632\t                routed_intent.intent_id,\n633\t                \"resolved\",\n634\t                \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n635\t            )\n636\t\n637\t            return {\n638\t                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n639\t                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n640\t                \"status\": \"unavailable\",\n641\t                \"message\": \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n642\t            }\n643\t\n644\t        try:\n645\t            # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n646\t            if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n647\t                result = await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent, timings)\n648\t            else:\n649\t                # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n650\t                result = await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent, timings)\n651\t        except Exception:\n652\t            # Persist whatever was captured before the failure, then re-raise.\n653\t            await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n654\t            raise\n655\t\n656\t        await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n657\t        return result\n658\t\n659\t    async def _persist_timings(\n660\t        self,\n661\t        intent_id: str,\n662\t        timings: DispatchTimings,\n663\t    ) -> None:\n664\t        \"\"\"Persist the captured dispatch timings. Non-fatal on error.\"\"\"\n665\t        try:\n666\t            store = await self._get_store()\n667\t            await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **timings.to_fields())\n668\t        except Exception as e:\n669\t            logger.warning(f\"dispatch timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n670\t\n671\t    async def _fetch_and_synthesize(\n672\t        self,\n673\t        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n674\t        timings: DispatchTimings,\n675\t    ) -> dict:\n676\t        \"\"\"\n677\t        Fetch context then synthesize into structured result.\n678\t\n679\t        Args:\n680\t            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n681\t            timings: Per-stage timing collector; records fetch_first_source_ms,\n682\t                fetch_total_ms, and synthesize_total_ms.\n683\t\n684\t        Returns:\n685\t            Result dictionary with synthesized data\n686\t        \"\"\"\n687\t        classification = routed_intent.classification\n688\t\n689\t        logger.info(\n690\t            f\"Fetching and synthesizing intent {routed_intent.intent_id} \"\n691\t            f\"(type: {classification.intent_type.value})\"\n692\t        )\n693\t\n694\t        try:\n695\t            # Step 1: Fetch context — resolve project slug to local repo path\n696\t            fetch_intent_type = self._map_intent_type(\n697\t                classification.intent_type,\n698\t                classification.lookup_kind\n699\t            )\n700\t\n701\t            from ..environment.discovery import get_registry\n702\t            from ..registry import get_project\n703\t            repo_path = None\n704\t            ssh_target = None\n705\t            host_alias = None\n706\t            registry = get_registry()\n707\t            if registry and classification.project_slug:\n708\t                entry = registry.lookup(classification.project_slug)\n709\t                if entry:\n710\t                    repo_path = str(entry.path)\n711\t                    ssh_target = entry.ssh_target\n712\t                    host_alias = entry.host\n713\t                    location = entry.display_path\n714\t                    logger.info(f\"Resolved '{classification.project_slug}' → {location}\")\n715\t                else:\n716\t                    logger.info(f\"No repo found for slug '{classification.project_slug}'\")\n717\t\n718\t            # YAML registry entry carries cluster/namespace/argocd_app — cluster\n719\t            # drives ArgoCD endpoint resolution (bead adc-1ejh: the fetch strand\n720\t            # resolves {argocd_api} from `cluster` via config/clusters.yaml).\n721\t            # argocd_app defaults to the slug when omitted (see _fetch_argocd_app).\n722\t            project_cfg = (\n723\t                get_project(classification.project_slug)\n724\t                if classification.project_slug else None\n725\t            )\n726\t\n727\t            fetch_request = FetchRequest(\n728\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n729\t                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n730\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n731\t                context=FetchContext(\n732\t                    project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n733\t                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n734\t                    repo_path=repo_path,\n735\t                    ssh_target=ssh_target,\n736\t                    host_alias=host_alias,\n737\t                    cluster=project_cfg.get(\"cluster\") if project_cfg else None,\n738\t                    namespace=project_cfg.get(\"namespace\") if project_cfg else None,\n739\t                    app_name=project_cfg.get(\"argocd_app\") if project_cfg else None,\n740\t                ),\n741\t            )\n742\t\n743\t            # fetch_first_source_ms = time from fetch start to the first source\n744\t            # resolving (success/fail/timeout — the first progress state on the\n745\t            # pending card). fetch_total_ms = the fetch window close.\n746\t            fetch_start = timings.clock()\n747\t            first_source_at: list[float | None] = [None]\n748\t            total_sources = len(get_fetch_commands(fetch_intent_type))\n749\t            completed_sources: list[int] = [0]\n750\t\n751\t            def _on_fetch_progress(source, result) -> None:\n752\t                \"\"\"Track fetch timing and broadcast progress events to pending card.\"\"\"\n753\t                # Track first source timing for fetch_first_source_ms metric\n754\t                if first_source_at[0] is None:\n755\t                    first_source_at[0] = timings.clock()\n756\t\n757\t                # Increment completed count\n758\t                completed_sources[0] += 1\n759\t\n760\t                # Broadcast progress event to canvas ('3/5 sources in')\n761\t                asyncio.create_task(broadcast_fetch_progress(\n762\t                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n763\t                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n764\t                    completed=completed_sources[0],\n765\t                    total=total_sources,\n766\t                    source_name=source.value,\n767\t                    source_status=result.status,\n768\t                ))\n769\t\n770\t            fetch_result = await execute_fetch(fetch_request, _on_fetch_progress)\n771\t            timings.record(\"fetch_total_ms\", fetch_result.total_duration_ms)\n772\t            if first_source_at[0] is not None:\n773\t                timings.record(\n774\t                    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n775\t                    timings.elapsed_ms(fetch_start, first_source_at[0]),\n776\t                )\n777\t\n778\t            # Check for terminal failure: ALL sources failed (Degraded-State UX)\n779\t            if fetch_result.terminal_failure == \"all_sources_failed\":\n780\t                logger.error(\n781\t                    f\"All fetch sources failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: \"\n782\t                    f\"{len(fetch_result.coverage.failed)} failed, \"\n783\t                    f\"{len(fetch_result.coverage.timed_out)} timed out\"\n784\t                )\n785\t\n786\t                # Build failed sources list for error event\n787\t                failed_sources = []\n788\t                for source, result in fetch_result.sources.items():\n789\t                    failed_sources.append({\n790\t                        \"source\": source.value,\n791\t                        \"status\": result.status,\n792\t                        \"error\": result.error,\n793\t                    })\n794\t\n795\t                # Broadcast all_sources_failed error event\n796\t                handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n797\t                await handler.broadcast_all_sources_failed(\n798\t                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n799\t                    intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n800\t                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n801\t                    utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n802\t                    failed_sources=failed_sources,\n803\t                )\n804\t\n805\t                # Update intent status to failed\n806\t                store = get_store()\n807\t                await store.update_intent_status(\n808\t                    routed_intent.intent_id,\n809\t                    \"failed\",\n810\t                    \"All fetch sources failed\",\n811\t                )\n812\t\n813\t                return {\n814\t                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n815\t                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n816\t                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n817\t                    \"error\": \"all_sources_failed\",\n818\t                    \"message\": \"No data — all required sources failed\",\n819\t                    \"terminal_failure\": \"all_sources_failed\",\n820\t                }\n821\t\n822\t            # Check for required source failures (terminal condition)\n823\t            required_sources_failed = [\n824\t                s for s in fetch_result.coverage.failed\n825\t Router - classifies utterances and routes to appropriate strands.\n\nUses LLM to classify intents by type and project, then routes:\n- task-profile intents → escalate strand (bead creation)\n- other intents → fetch + synthesize strands\n\"\"\"\nimport asyncio\nimport json\nimport time\nimport uuid\nimport re\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom enum import Enum\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any, Optional\nfrom functools import lru_cache\n\nimport httpx\n\nfrom ..components.hot_reload import get_reload_manager\nfrom ..escalate.handler import EscalateRequest, escalate_intent\nfrom ..escalate.llm import get_zai_client, ModelClass, LLMTimeoutError, LLMRateLimitError, LLMError\nfrom ..errors.degraded_state import get_degraded_state_handler\nfrom ..instrument.timings import DispatchTimings\nfrom ..llm.response_parser import parse_llm_response, ParseLLMError\nfrom ..render.hot_path import derive_result_type, get_renderer\nfrom ..session.store import get_store\nfrom ..fetch.commands import FetchRequest, FetchContext, IntentType as FetchIntentType, get_fetch_commands\nfrom ..fetch.orchestrator import execute_fetch\nfrom ..synthesize.strand import SynthesizeRequest, synthesize_intent\nfrom ..sse.broadcaster import get_broadcaster, SSEEvent, EventType, broadcast_fetch_progress\n\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n# Router response cache - LRU cache for repeated utterances within a session\n# Key: (utterance_hash, session_id), Value: (classifications, timestamp)\n# 15-minute TTL for cache entries - user intent doesn't change frequently in conversation\n_ROUTER_CACHE: dict[tuple[str, str], tuple[list, float]] = {}\n_CACHE_TTL = 900  # 15 minutes - longer TTL for better hit rate in conversation flow\n_MAX_CACHE_SIZE = 1000  # Increased capacity for more cache hits\n\n\n# Router error types for degraded-state handling\nclass RouterError(Exception):\n    \"\"\"Base exception for router errors.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass RouterTimeoutError(RouterError):\n    \"\"\"Router LLM call timed out.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass RouterQuotaError(RouterError):\n    \"\"\"Router LLM call quota exhausted.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass RouterProxyError(RouterError):\n    \"\"\"Router LLM proxy unreachable.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass RouterMalformedError(RouterError):\n    \"\"\"Router returned malformed JSON (after corrective retry).\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, parse_error: str, raw_output: str, retry_count: int = 0):\n        self.parse_error = parse_error\n        self.raw_output = raw_output\n        self.retry_count = retry_count\n        super().__init__(f\"Malformed router output (retry {retry_count}): {parse_error}\")\n\n\nclass IntentType(Enum):\n    \"\"\"Intent types that the router can classify.\"\"\"\n    STATUS = \"status\"\n    ACTION = \"action\"\n    BRAINSTORM = \"brainstorm\"\n    LOOKUP = \"lookup\"\n    REMINDER = \"reminder\"\n    SELF_MODIFICATION = \"self-modification\"\n    MONITORING_CONFIG = \"monitoring-config\"\n    TASK_PROFILE = \"task-profile\"  # Escalate to NEEDLE bead\n    CLARIFICATION = \"clarification\"  # Needs user input\n    STUCK = \"stuck\"  # Stuck intent - task blocked by circuit breaker\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass IntentClassification:\n    \"\"\"Result of intent classification.\"\"\"\n    intent_type: IntentType\n    project_slug: str | None = None\n    confidence: float = 1.0\n    utterance_fragment: str = \"\"\n    reasoning: str = \"\"\n    urgency: str = \"normal\"  # critical, high, normal, low\n    lookup_kind: str | None = None  # lookup intents only: 'logs' | 'config' | 'docs'\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass RoutedIntent:\n    \"\"\"A routed intent ready for processing.\"\"\"\n    intent_id: str\n    classification: IntentClassification\n    session_id: str\n    utterance: str\n    # router_ms is measured once around classify_utterance() in route_utterance\n    # (one LLM call per utterance) and shared across every intent thread from\n    # that utterance — see Latency Budget & Instrumentation in docs/plan/plan.md.\n    router_ms: int | None = None\n\n\n# Path to the router segmentation prompt. Read from disk on each classify_utterance()\n# call so edits to prompts/router.md take effect without a server restart (hot-reload),\n# matching the pattern in src/synthesize/strand.py (SYNTHESIZE_PROMPT_PATH).\nROUTER_PROMPT_PATH = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/prompts/router.md\")\n\n# Fallback used only if the prompt file cannot be read at runtime.\n_ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK = (\n    \"You are the Intent Router for aide-de-camp. Segment the utterance into \"\n    \"distinct intent threads, classify each, and return ONLY a JSON array of \"\n    \"intent objects.\"\n)\n\n\nclass IntentRouter:\n    \"\"\"\n    Intent Router classifies utterances and routes to appropriate strands.\n\n    For task-profile intents, routes to escalate strand for bead creation.\n    For other intents, routes to fetch + synthesize strands (TODO).\n\n    Latency optimizations:\n    - LRU cache for repeated utterances (5-minute TTL)\n    - Reduced max_tokens (128 vs 256) for faster generation\n    - Dedicated 10s timeout for fail-fast behavior\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, store=None, prompt_path: Optional[Path] = None):\n        self.store = store\n        self.prompt_path = prompt_path or ROUTER_PROMPT_PATH\n        self._zai_client = None\n        self._router_zai_client = None  # Dedicated client with 10s timeout\n        self._reload_manager = None\n\n    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create ZAI client.\"\"\"\n        if self._zai_client is None:\n            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n        return self._zai_client\n\n    async def _get_router_zai_client(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create dedicated ZAI client for router with optimized settings for low latency.\"\"\"\n        if self._router_zai_client is None:\n            from ..escalate.llm import get_router_zai_client\n            # Router needs fail-fast behavior with aggressive timeout\n            # 8-second timeout allows: ~2s connection + ~4-5s inference + ~1s parsing\n            self._router_zai_client = get_router_zai_client(timeout=8.0)\n        return self._router_zai_client\n\n    def _get_utterance_hash(self, utterance: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Generate hash key for utterance caching.\"\"\"\n        import hashlib\n        return hashlib.md5(utterance.encode()).hexdigest()\n\n    def _get_cached_classification(self, utterance: str, session_id: str) -> list[IntentClassification] | None:\n        \"\"\"Check cache for existing classification.\"\"\"\n        utterance_hash = self._get_utterance_hash(utterance)\n        cache_key = (utterance_hash, session_id)\n\n        if cache_key in _ROUTER_CACHE:\n            classifications, timestamp = _ROUTER_CACHE[cache_key]\n            age = time.time() - timestamp\n            if age < _CACHE_TTL:\n                logger.info(f\"Router cache HIT for utterance hash {utterance_hash[:8]} (age: {age:.1f}s)\")\n                return classifications\n            else:\n                # Remove expired entry\n                del _ROUTER_CACHE[cache_key]\n                logger.info(f\"Router cache EXPIRED for utterance hash {utterance_hash[:8]}\")\n\n        return None\n\n    def _cache_classification(self, utterance: str, session_id: str, classifications: list[IntentClassification]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Cache classification result.\"\"\"\n        # Prune cache if at capacity\n        if len(_ROUTER_CACHE) >= _MAX_CACHE_SIZE:\n            # Remove oldest entry (first in dict)\n            oldest_key = next(iter(_ROUTER_CACHE))\n            del _ROUTER_CACHE[oldest_key]\n            logger.info(f\"Router cache pruned oldest entry (size: {len(_ROUTER_CACHE)})\")\n\n        utterance_hash = self._get_utterance_hash(utterance)\n        cache_key = (utterance_hash, session_id)\n        _ROUTER_CACHE[cache_key] = (classifications, time.time())\n        logger.info(f\"Router cache STORED utterance hash {utterance_hash[:8]} (cache size: {len(_ROUTER_CACHE)})\")\n\n    async def _get_store(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n        if self.store is None:\n            self.store = get_store()\n        return self.store\n\n    def _get_reload_manager(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create the hot-reload manager (lazy singleton).\"\"\"\n        if self._reload_manager is None:\n            self._reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n        return self._reload_manager\n\n    def _load_router_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Load the router segmentation prompt via hot-reload manager.\n\n        Uses the cached prompt from the hot-reload manager, which only reloads\n        when the file mtime changes. This avoids disk I/O on every classification.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            return self._get_reload_manager().get_prompt(\"router\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to load router prompt: {e}\")\n            return _ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK\n\n    def _build_system_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Build the system prompt from the router segmentation prompt.\n\n        Latency optimization: Removed urgency prompt splicing from hot path.\n        The router still classifies urgency based on core intent patterns,\n        without the extra token overhead of separate urgency rules.\n        \"\"\"\n        return self._load_router_prompt()\n\n    async def classify_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        retry_on_malformed: bool = True,\n    ) -> list[IntentClassification]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Classify an utterance into intents.\n\n        Uses LLM to segment and classify the utterance.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            session_id: Session ID for context\n            retry_on_malformed: If True, perform one corrective retry on JSON parse failure\n\n        Returns:\n            List of IntentClassification objects\n\n        Raises:\n            RouterTimeoutError: LLM call timed out\n            RouterQuotaError: LLM quota exhausted\n            RouterProxyError: LLM proxy unreachable\n            RouterMalformedError: Malformed JSON after corrective retry\n        \"\"\"\n        # OPTIMIZATION 1: Check cache first (eliminates redundant LLM calls)\n        cached_result = self._get_cached_classification(utterance, session_id)\n        if cached_result is not None:\n            # Return empty timing breakdown for cached results (no actual LLM call was made)\n            empty_breakdown = {\n                \"cached\": True,\n                \"prompt_construction_ms\": 0,\n                \"proxy_call_ms\": 0,\n                \"proxy_network_ms\": 0,\n                \"proxy_inference_ms\": 0,\n                \"json_parse_ms\": 0,\n                \"process_ms\": 0,\n                \"total_ms\": 0,\n                \"intents_count\": len(cached_result),\n            }\n            return cached_result, empty_breakdown\n\n        # Track retry attempt for malformed JSON\n        retry_count = 0\n        raw_response = None\n\n        try:\n            # Measure total classification time\n            classify_start = time.perf_counter()\n\n            # Use dedicated router client with 10s timeout for fail-fast behavior\n            client = await self._get_router_zai_client()\n\n            # Measure prompt construction time (template rendering, message formatting)\n            # This isolates client-side preparation cost from network and inference time\n            prompt_start = time.perf_counter()\n\n            # Build user message\n            user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}\"\n\n            logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n\n            # Build system prompt from prompts/router.md\n            # (hot-reload enabled via reload manager)\n            system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt()\n\n            prompt_ms = (time.perf_counter() - prompt_start) * 1000\n\n            # Measure ZAI proxy call time (network + model inference)\n            # The LLM client now returns timing breakdown separating network latency from model inference\n            proxy_start = time.perf_counter()\n            response_data = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n                max_tokens=128,  # OPTIMIZATION 2: Reduced from 256 to 128 - intent JSON is typically 50-80 tokens\n                temperature=0.0,\n                return_timing=True,  # Request timing breakdown from LLM client\n            )\n            proxy_ms = (time.perf_counter() - proxy_start) * 1000\n\n            # Extract content and timing from response\n            # response_data is now a dict with content and timing breakdown\n            response = response_data.get(\"content\", \"\")\n            timing_network_ms = response_data.get(\"timing_network_ms\")\n            timing_inference_ms = response_data.get(\"timing_inference_ms\")\n\n            # Store raw response for error reporting\n            raw_response = response\n\n            # Calculate model inference time: proxy response time - network time\n            # This gives us the actual model inference duration by subtracting network RTT\n            calculated_inference_ms = None\n            if timing_network_ms is not None:\n                calculated_inference_ms = proxy_ms - timing_network_ms\n\n            # Measure JSON parsing time\n            parse_start = time.perf_counter()\n            try:\n                # Optimized JSON parsing using centralized parser\n                # Uses single-pass find()/rfind() approach (faster than split/rsplit)\n                # Handles GLM-4.7's markdown-fenced responses efficiently\n                # See src/llm/response_parser.py for implementation\n                intents_data = parse_llm_response(response, strip_fences=True, expect_json=True)\n            except ParseLLMError as e:\n                # Already has raw_response attached; just re-raise\n                raise e\n            parse_ms = (time.perf_counter() - parse_start) * 1000\n\n            # Measure classification processing time\n            process_start = time.perf_counter()\n            classifications = []\n\n            for intent_data in intents_data:\n                # Map string to IntentType enum\n                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n                try:\n                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n                except ValueError:\n                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n\n                classification = IntentClassification(\n                    intent_type=intent_type,\n                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n                    lookup_kind=intent_data.get(\"lookup_kind\") if intent_type == IntentType.LOOKUP else None,\n                )\n                classifications.append(classification)\n            process_ms = (time.perf_counter() - process_start) * 1000\n\n            total_ms = (time.perf_counter() - classify_start) * 1000\n\n            # Log detailed timing breakdown for latency profiling\n            # This shows WHERE the 1,587-2,074ms latency is spent across the 4 investigation areas\n            network_str = f\"{timing_network_ms:.2f}\" if timing_network_ms is not None else \"N/A\"\n            calculated_inference_str = f\"{calculated_inference_ms:.2f}\" if calculated_inference_ms is not None else \"N/A\"\n            logger.info(\n                f\"router_timing breakdown: \"\n                f\"prompt_construction_ms={prompt_ms:.2f} \"\n                f\"proxy_call_ms={proxy_ms:.2f} \"\n                f\"proxy_network_ms={network_str} \"\n                f\"proxy_inference_ms={calculated_inference_str} \"\n                f\"json_parse_ms={parse_ms:.2f} \"\n                f\"process_ms={process_ms:.2f} \"\n                f\"total_ms={total_ms:.2f} \"\n                f\"intents={len(classifications)}\"\n            )\n\n            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n\n            # Build timing breakdown for storage\n            # Now includes separate network and inference timing measured by LLM client\n            timing_breakdown = {\n                \"prompt_construction_ms\": round(prompt_ms, 2),\n                \"proxy_call_ms\": round(proxy_ms, 2),  # Total round-trip time (network + inference)\n                \"proxy_network_ms\": round(timing_network_ms, 2) if timing_network_ms is not None else None,\n                \"proxy_inference_ms\": round(calculated_inference_ms, 2) if calculated_inference_ms is not None else None,\n                \"json_parse_ms\": round(parse_ms, 2),\n                \"process_ms\": round(process_ms, 2),\n                \"total_ms\": round(total_ms, 2),\n                \"intents_count\": len(classifications),\n            }\n\n            # OPTIMIZATION 1 (cont): Cache successful classifications\n            self._cache_classification(utterance, session_id, classifications)\n\n            return classifications, timing_breakdown\n\n        except ParseLLMError as e:\n            \"\"\"\n            First parsing attempt failed - implement corrective retry.\n\n            Router uses corrective retry because it's the first step in the pipeline.\n            A malformed router response cascades to everything downstream.\n            One retry is justified because it's cheap (same prompt, 2048 tokens).\n            \"\"\"\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response: {e}\")\n            raw_output = e.raw_response or raw_response  # Preserve for error reporting\n\n            # Implement one corrective retry on malformed JSON\n            if retry_on_malformed and retry_count == 0:\n                logger.info(\"Malformed JSON detected, attempting corrective retry...\")\n                retry_count += 1\n                try:\n                    # Retry once with same parameters\n                    classifications, _ = await self.classify_utterance(\n                        utterance=utterance,\n                        session_id=session_id,\n                        retry_on_malformed=False,  # Prevent infinite retry\n                    )\n                    return classifications, _\n                except ParseLLMError as retry_e:\n                    # Retry also failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n                    raise RouterMalformedError(\n                        parse_error=str(retry_e),\n                        raw_output=retry_e.raw_response or raw_response,\n                        retry_count=retry_count,\n                    ) from retry_e\n\n            # No retry allowed or retry failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n            raise RouterMalformedError(\n                parse_error=str(e),\n                raw_output=raw_output,\n                retry_count=retry_count,\n            ) from e\n\n        except (LLMTimeoutError, asyncio.TimeoutError) as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\")\n            raise RouterTimeoutError(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\") from e\n\n        except (LLMRateLimitError,) as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\")\n            raise RouterQuotaError(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\") from e\n\n        except (LLMError, httpx.HTTPError) as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\")\n            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\") from e\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Classification failed with unexpected error: {e}\")\n            # Wrap unknown errors as RouterProxyError for graceful degradation\n            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM call failed: {e}\") from e\n\n    async def route_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        utterance_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n\n        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n            session_id: Session ID\n\n        Returns:\n            List of RoutedIntent objects\n\n        Raises:\n            RouterError: If router fails and broadcasts error event\n        \"\"\"\n        # Generate a fallback intent_id for error reporting\n        error_intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n        try:\n            # Classify the utterance (one LLM call per utterance — the router stage\n            # is shared across every intent thread it produces, so its duration is\n            # measured once here and stamped onto each RoutedIntent).\n            classify_start = time.monotonic()\n            classifications, timing_breakdown = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n            router_ms = int((time.monotonic() - classify_start) * 1000)\n\n            # Store router timing breakdown in utterance record\n            try:\n                store = await self._get_store()\n                await store.update_utterance_router_timing(utterance_id, timing_breakdown)\n                logger.info(f\"Stored router timing breakdown for utterance {utterance_id[:8]}\")\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.warning(f\"Failed to store router timing breakdown: {e}\")\n                # Non-fatal: continue with routing\n\n            routed_intents = []\n            for classification in classifications:\n                # Create intent ID\n                intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n                routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n                    intent_id=intent_id,\n                    classification=classification,\n                    session_id=session_id,\n                    utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n                    router_ms=router_ms,\n                )\n                routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n\n            return routed_intents\n\n        except RouterMalformedError as e:\n            # Malformed JSON after corrective retry - broadcast clarification card\n            logger.error(f\"Router malformed after retry: {e.parse_error}\")\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_clarification_card(\n                utterance=utterance,\n                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                parse_error=e.parse_error,\n                retry_count=e.retry_count,\n                raw_output_snippet=e.raw_output[:200] if e.raw_output else None,\n            )\n            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n\n        except RouterTimeoutError as e:\n            # Router LLM timeout - broadcast router_unavailable\n            logger.error(f\"Router timeout: {e}\")\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n                utterance=utterance,\n                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                error_reason=\"timeout\",\n            )\n            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n\n        except RouterQuotaError as e:\n            # Router quota exhausted - broadcast router_unavailable\n            logger.error(f\"Router quota exhausted: {e}\")\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n                utterance=utterance,\n                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                error_reason=\"quota_exhausted\",\n            )\n            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n\n        except RouterProxyError as e:\n            # Router proxy unreachable - broadcast router_unavailable\n            logger.error(f\"Router proxy error: {e}\")\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n                utterance=utterance,\n                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                error_reason=\"proxy_down\",\n            )\n            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n\n    async def process_intent(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with status and data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        # Capture per-stage timings for this dispatch and persist one\n        # dispatch_timings row (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Each\n        # branch records the stages it measures; router_ms is shared across\n        # the utterance's threads and was stamped on the RoutedIntent. Capture\n        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\n        # the dispatch itself.\n        timings = DispatchTimings()\n        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n\n        # Honesty guards for unimplemented intents\n        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.ACTION:\n            # Action executor is NOT BUILT — broadcast design-only card\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_action_design_only(\n                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n            )\n\n            # Update intent status to reflect the design-only state\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.update_intent_status(\n                routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"resolved\",\n                \"Action execution is design-only — executor not built\",\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"design_only\",\n                \"message\": \"Action execution is not yet available\",\n            }\n\n        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.REMINDER:\n            # Reminders are NOT YET IMPLEMENTED — broadcast clarification card\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_reminder_unavailable(\n                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n\n            # Update intent status to reflect that reminders are unavailable\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.update_intent_status(\n                routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"resolved\",\n                \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"unavailable\",\n                \"message\": \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n            }\n\n        try:\n            # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n            if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n                result = await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent, timings)\n            else:\n                # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n                result = await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent, timings)\n        except Exception:\n            # Persist whatever was captured before the failure, then re-raise.\n            await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n            raise\n\n        await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n        return result\n\n    async def _persist_timings(\n        self,\n        intent_id: str,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Persist the captured dispatch timings. Non-fatal on error.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **timings.to_fields())\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"dispatch timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n\n    async def _fetch_and_synthesize(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Fetch context then synthesize into structured result.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n            timings: Per-stage timing collector; records fetch_first_source_ms,\n                fetch_total_ms, and synthesize_total_ms.\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with synthesized data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetching and synthesizing intent {routed_intent.intent_id} \"\n            f\"(type: {classification.intent_type.value})\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Step 1: Fetch context — resolve project slug to local repo path\n            fetch_intent_type =            if classification.project_slug else None\n            )\n\n            fetch_request = FetchRequest(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n first_source_at: list[float | None] = [None]\n            total_sources = len(get_fetch_commands(fetch_intent_type))\n            completed_sources: list[int] = [0]\n\n            def _on_fetch_progress(source, result) -> None:\n                \"\"\"Track fetch timing and broadcast progress events to pending card.\"\"\"\n                # Track first source timing for fetch_first_source_ms metric\n                if first_source_at[0] is None:\n                    first_source_at[0] = timings.clock()\n\n                # Increment completed count\n                completed_sources[0] += 1\n\n                # Broadcast progress event to canvas ('3/5 sources in')\n                asyncio.create_task(broadcast_fetch_progress(\n                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    completed=completed_sources[0],\n                    total=total_sources,\n                    source_name=source.value,\n                    source_status=result.status,\n                ))\n\n            fetch_result = await execute_fetch(fetch_request, _on_fetch_progress)\n            timings.record(\"fetch_total_ms\", fetch_result.total_duration_ms)\n            if first_source_at[0] is not None:\n                timings.record(\n                    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n                    timings.elapsed_ms(fetch_start, first_source_at[0]),\n                )\n\n            # Check for terminal failure: ALL sources failed (Degraded-State UX)\n            if event\n                failed_sources = []\n                for source, result in fetch_result.sources.items():\n                    failed_sources.append({\n                        \"source\":           routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"failed\",\n                    \"All fetch sources failed\",\n                )\n\n                return {\n                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n                    \"error\": lookup_kind: str | None = None) -> FetchIntentType:\n        \"\"\"Map router IntentType to fetch IntentType.\n\n        For lookup intents, route to the subtype-specific fetch matrix based on lookup_kind.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Map enum values by string\n        type_map = {\n            IntentType.STATUS: FetchIntentType.STATUS,\n            IntentType.ACTION: FetchIntentType.ACTION,\n            IntentType.BRAINSTORM: FetchIntentType.BRAINSTORM,\n            IntentType.LOOKUP: FetchIntentType.LOOKUP,\n            IntentType.REMINDER: FetchIntentType.REMINDER,\n            IntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION: FetchIntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION,\n            IntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG: FetchIntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG,\n            IntentType.STUCK: FetchIntentType.STUCK,\n        }\n\n        # For lookup intents with lookup_kind, route to the subtype-specific matrix\n        if intent_type == IntentType.LOOKUP and lookup_kind:\n            if lookup_kind == \"logs\":\n                return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_LOGS\n            elif lookup_kind == \"config\":\n                return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_CONFIG\n            elif lookup_kind == \"docs\":\n                return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_DOCS\n\n        return type_map.get(intent_type, FetchIntentType.STATUS)\n\n    async def _check_fence_for_bead(\n        self,\n        bead_ref: str,\n    ) -> dict | None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Check if a bead has been fenced (has last_refusal_reason or fenced_at set).\n\n        Args:\n            bead_ref: The bead reference to check\n\n        Returns:\n            Fence context dict with bead_id, refusal_reason, refusal_count, fenced_at\n            if fenced, None return None\n\n            # Check if bead is fenced (has last_refusal_reason or fenced_at)\n            last_refusal_reason = bead_watch.get(\"last_refusal_reason\")\n            fenced_at = bead_watch.get(\"fenced_at\")\n            refusal_count = bead_watch.get(\"refusal_count\", 0)\n\n            if last_refusal_reason or fenced_at:\n                # Bead is fenced - extract fence context\n                return {\n                    \"bead_id\": bead_ref,\n                    \"refusal_reason\": last_refusal_reason or \"Fenced (no reason provided)\",\n                    \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                    \"fenced_at\": fenced_at,\n                }\n\n            return None\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Error checking fence status for bead {bead_ref}: {e}\")\n            return None\n\n    async def _create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        fence_context: f\"Creating stuck card for fenced bead {bead_id} \"\n            f\"(intent {routed_intent.intent_id})\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n\n            # Update          status=\"stuck\",\n            )\n\n            # Get or create topic for this stuck card\n            topic_type = \"project\" if routed_intent.classification.project_slug else task has been blocked after {refusal_count} refusals.\",\n                \"action_hint\": \"Review the bead and provide the missing information or context needed to proceed.\",\n                \"fence_detected_during\": \"intent_routing\",\n            }\n\n            # Derive result_type from original intent classification (not \"stuck\" state, includes lookup_kind)\n            result_type = derive_result_type(\n                intent_type=routed_intent.classification.intent_type.value,\n                project_slug=routed_intent.classification.project_slug,\n                lookup_kind=routed_intent.classification.lookup_kind,\n            )\n\n            result_id = await store.create_result(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                summary=summary,\n                data=data,\n                urgency=\"high\",\n                result_type=result_type,\n            )\n\n            # Render stuck card via hot-path selector (deterministic, no LLM)\n            renderer = get_renderer()\n            render_outcome = urgency=\"high\",\n            )\n\n            # Update result's card_fallback flag\n            await store.update_result_card_fallback(result_id, render_outcome.card_fallback)\n\n broadcaster.broadcast(\n                SSEEvent(\n                    event_type=EventType.TASK_STUCK,\n                    data={\n                        \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n session before escalating\n        # This detects fence events when last_refusal_reason is set on existing beads\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            fenced_beads = \"fenced_at\": most_recent_fenced.get(\"fenced_at\"),\n                }\n\n                logger.info(\n                    f\"Detected fenced bead {bead_ref} for session {routed_intent.session_id} self._create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n                    routed_intent, fence_context\n                )\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Error checking for fenced beads: {e}\")\n            # Continue to escalation if fence check fails\n\n        # Step 2: No fenced beads detected - proceed with escalation\n        # Build escalate request\n        escalate_request = EscalateRequest(\n            intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n            session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n            intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n            project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n            context={\n                \"reasoning\": classification.reasoning,\n                \"urgency\": classification.urgency,\n            },\n            metadata={\n                \"urgency\": classification.urgency,\n                \"confidence\": classification.confidence,\n            },\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Escalate to bead — escalate_ms budgets formulation + validation +\n            # bf create (task-profile dispatches only; NULL for hot-path rows).\n            esc_start = timings.clock()\n            result = await escalate_intent(escalate_request)\n            timings.record(\"escalate_ms\", timings.elapsed_ms(esc_start))\n\n            # Check if the created bead is immediately fenced (race condition:\n            # watcher may have fenced it between escalate request and now)\n            if result.bead_id:\n                fence_context = self._create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n                        routed_intent, fence_context\n                    )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"escalated\",\n                \"bead_id\": result.bead_id,\n                \"pending_card\": result.pending_card,\n                \"message\": f\"Escalated to bead {result.bead_id}\",\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Escalation failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {e}\")\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n46\t);\n47\t\n48\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_surfaces_session ON surfaces(session_id);\n49\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_surfaces_state ON surfaces(state);\n50\t\n51\t-- Utterances: raw input from user\n52\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS utterances (\n53\t    id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n54\t    session_id  TEXT NOT NULL,\n55\t    raw_text    TEXT NOT NULL,\n56\t    created_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n57\t    router_timing_breakdown TEXT,  -- JSON: detailed timing breakdown from intent router (prompt_ms, proxy_ms, parse_ms, process_ms, total_ms)\n58\t    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n59\t);\n60\t\n61\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_utterances_session ON utterances(session_id);\n62\t\n63\t-- NULL,\n68\t    topic_id     TEXT,\n69\t    project_slug TEXT,\n70\t    intent_type  TEXT NOT NULL,\n71\t    lookup_kind  TEXT,  -- lookup intents only: 'logs' | 'config' | 'docs' (router-emitted; see Intent Router). NULL otherwise\n72\t    status       TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(status IN ('pending', 'dispatched', 'resolved', 'cancelled')) DEFAULT 'pending',\n73\t    bead_ref     TEXT,\n74\t    created_at   INTEGER NOT NULL,\n75\t    resolved_at  INTEGER,\n76\t    FOREIGN KEY (utterance_id) REFERENCES utterances(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n77\t    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n78\t    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n79\t);\n80\t\n81\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_intents_session ON intents(session_id);\n82\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_intents_topic ON intents(topic_id);\n83\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_intents_status ON intents(status);\n84\t\n85\t-- Results: structured data returned by agents\n86\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS results (\n87\t    id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n88\t    intent_id   TEXT,  -- NULL for monitoring-originated results (system-originated, no utterance)\n89\t    topic_id    TEXT NOT NULL,\n90\t    session_id  TEXT NOT NULL,\n91\t    summary     TEXT NOT NULL,\n92\t    data        TEXT NOT NULL,  -- JSON\n93\t    urgency     TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(urgency IN ('critical', 'high', 'normal', 'low')) DEFAULT 'normal',\n94\t    result_type TEXT,  -- deterministic card-selector key, set at result-write time:\n95\t                       -- \"{intent_type}:{project_slug}\" for intent-derived results — one per\n96\t                       -- intent thread (the aggregated thread card); lookup threads insert the\n97\t                       -- intent's lookup_kind: \"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{project_slug}\";\n98\t                       -- \"monitoring:{project_slug}\" for monitoring-originated rows. Library → Built-in generic fallback card). 0 when a real component rendered it (card_cache holds the rendered HTML).\n102\t    created_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n103\t    surfaced_at INTEGER,\n104\t    acked_at    INTEGER,\n105\t    previous_result_id TEXT,  -- Link to previous result for diff\n106\t    diff_summary TEXT,  -- Human-readable diff summary\n107\t    diff_data    TEXT,  -- JSON: detailed field diffs\n108\t    FOREIGN KEY (intent_id) REFERENCES intents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n109\t    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n110\t    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n111\t    FOREIGN KEY (previous_result_id) REFERENCES results(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n112\t);\n113\t\n114\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS   TEXT NOT NULL,  -- JSON: signal-specific data\n166\t    surface_type TEXT,\n167\t    processed    INTEGER DEFAULT 0 CHECK(processed IN (0, 1)),\n168\t    processed_at INTEGER,\n169\t    FOREIGN KEY feedback_signals(result_id);\n178\t\n179\t-- Per-stage latency capture for every dispatch (Latency Budget & Instrumentation).\n180\t-- Keyed by the intent *thread* id (routed_intent.intent_id — the same id the\n181\t-- fetch/synthesize/escalate strands and the results row use), NOT the\n182\t-- intents.id the caller mints in create_intent(). router_ms is shared across\n183\t-- every intent      INTEGER,\n196\t    sse_emit_ms               INTEGER,\n197\t    stt_ms                    INTEGER,\n198\t    first_render_ms           INTEGER,\n199\t    created_at                INTEGER NOT NULL\n200\t);\n201\t\n202\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dispatch_timings_created ON dispatch_timings(created_at);\n203\t\n204\t-- Bead watch table: circuit breaker tracking for async beads\n205\t-- deadlines, and fencing state. All state is persisted\n208\t-- so a watcher restart loses nothing -- breaker state lives in the database,\n209\t-- not only in memory.\n210\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS bead_watch (\n211\t    bead_ref           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,  -- References bead ID (intents.bead_ref)\n212\t    refusal_count      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,  -- Number of REFUSED: comments seen\n213\t    last_refusal_reason TEXT,  -- Most recent refusal reason\n214\t    last_refusal_at    INTEGER,  -- Timestamp of most recent refusal\n215\t    comment_high_water INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT -1,  -- Latest comment index processed (-1 = none)\n216\t    sla_deadline       INTEGER NOT NULL,  -- Unix timestamp when SLA expires\n217\t    sla_flagged_at     INTEGER,  -- Timestamp when SLA was flagged (NULL if not flagged)\n218\t bead_watch(fenced_at);\n224\t\n225\t-- Pending bead approvals: beads awaiting user approval before creation\n226\t-- Stores approval card data for action/self_modification/monitoring_config beads\n227\t-- that passed validation but require explicit user approval.\n228\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS pending_bead_approvals (\n229\t    id                  TEXT PRIMARY KEY,  -- UUID for this validation_result   TEXT NOT NULL,  -- JSON: ValidationResult with approval details\n235\t    utterance           TEXT NOT NULL,  -- Original user utterance\n236\t    project_slug        TEXT,  -- Optional project slug\n237\t    topic_id            TEXT,  -- Optional topic ID\n238\t    created_at          INTEGER NOT NULL,  -- When the approval was requested\n239\t    expires_at          INTEGER NOT NULL,  -- When this approval request expires\n240\t    status              TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(status IN ('pending', 'approved', 'rejected')) DEFAULT 'pending',\n241\t    FOREIGN KEY
